faqs.question: What causes drain fly infestations in Sheepshead Bay homes?
faqs.answer: Drain flies breed in the organic matter that accumulates inside drains, pipes, and sewage systems. In Sheepshead Bay, older buildings with aging plumbing and moisture-rich environments near the waterfront are particularly susceptible. Standing water, slow drains, and infrequently used sinks or floor drains create ideal breeding conditions for these pests.

faqs.question: How long does drain fly treatment take in Sheepshead Bay?
faqs.answer: Most drain fly treatments in Sheepshead Bay take 1-2 hours depending on the severity of the infestation and the number of drains affected. Our technicians conduct a thorough inspection, treat all affected drains with bio-enzymatic cleaners and approved insecticides, and provide prevention recommendations. Results are typically visible within 24-48 hours.

faqs.question: Is drain fly treatment safe for my family and pets?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved products and eco-friendly bio-enzymatic treatments that are safe for families and pets when applied correctly. Our technicians follow strict safety protocols and will advise you on any necessary precautions during and after treatment. Most treatments allow you to remain in your home during the service.

faqs.question: What's included in the $350-$550 drain fly treatment service?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive service includes a full property inspection to identify all breeding sites, treatment of all affected drains with professional-grade products, removal of organic buildup, application of residual treatments, and a follow-up visit if needed.
